title: Land Reclamation! - Marginal REVOLUTION
gist: The 'they aren't making any more land' adage is historically false: humans have reclaimed enormous land areas, and the vast majority of reclamation is for farmland via wetland drainage rather than urban fill.

insights.claim: Half the land area of Boston, a quarter of Manhattan, and 15% of San Francisco were raised from the sea before 1970, contradicting the notion that land supply is fixed.
insights.speaker: Alex Tabarrok
insights.so_what: Coastal land supply is more elastic than 'buy land, they aren't making more' assumes; reclamation is a latent lever on urban real estate scarcity.
insights.quote: Half the land area of Boston, a quarter of Manhattan, and 15% of San Francisco were raised from the sea before 1970.

insights.claim: US wetlands fell from 221 million acres in the 1780s to 104 million by the 1980s, roughly 117 million acres drained at 60 acres per hour sustained for 200 years, exceeding the entire ~70 million acre US urban footprint.
insights.speaker: Alex Tabarrok
insights.so_what: Most 'made' land became farmland, not cities; agricultural land supply has been vastly expanded historically, a lens for thinking about future food-supply elasticity.
insights.quote: America has drained substantially more wetland than it has built city, and nearly all of that drained land became farmland.

themes.theme: Reclamation primarily creates farmland, not cities

insights.claim: The Netherlands, with the most reclaimed land per person, devotes about two-thirds of its dry land to farmland and ranks as the world's second-largest agricultural exporter, with Flevoland's 1,410 sq km reclaimed from the Zuiderzee laid out as an agricultural basin.
insights.speaker: Alex Tabarrok
insights.so_what: High-value reclaimed land is deployed to low-value agriculture in the Dutch model, a signal about how land-use regulation and legacy planning constrain optimal allocation.
insights.quote: The country with the most reclaimed land per person uses it to grow potatoes, graze dairy cattle, and ranks as the world's second-largest agricultural exporter.
insights.locator: The Dutch invented the modern polder

insights.claim: Land was historically drained to eliminate mosquito-driven malaria and improve sewage, as with the Potomac Flats where 1882 dredging created 600+ acres now holding the Lincoln and Jefferson Memorials.
insights.speaker: Alex Tabarrok
insights.so_what: Public-health motives, not just real estate, historically drove reclamation; a reminder that infrastructure value bundles disease control and land creation.
insights.locator: In the mid-1800s the land south and west
